text.title: Analytics Setup Agent Rules and Gotchas
text.summary: Agent-specific rules for analytics install, GTM, Measurement Protocol, and common LLM failure modes in analytics implementation.

rule.statement: Call gtag('config', ...) before gtag('event', ...) in all page load sequences; events queued before config runs are sent to the wrong property.
rule.rationale: gtag processes calls sequentially. An event fired before the config call has no property destination and silently drops. This commonly happens in SPA route-change handlers that fire before the config tag has loaded.

rule.statement: For cross-domain tracking, configure the linker parameter explicitly; cross-domain sessions break silently after redesigns without obvious error messages.
rule.rationale: Without linker configuration, users crossing domains create new sessions and appear as new users from "direct" traffic. The conversion gets attributed to the wrong channel.

rule.statement: Export and version-control the GTM container JSON; containers exported across accounts have secrets stripped and require manual re-linking of API keys.
rule.rationale: GTM container exports are the only backup for tracking configuration. Without version control, a misconfigured container deployment has no rollback path.

antipattern.description: Firing page_view events in single-page apps before the URL route is settled.
antipattern.reason: GTM fires on history change events immediately. In some frameworks, the route update completes asynchronously after the history push. Early fires record the previous URL as the page_view location.

rule.statement: Server-side Measurement Protocol events require the client_id matching the gtag-set first-party cookie; events without a matching client_id create orphaned sessions and inflate session counts.
rule.rationale: GA4 stitches sessions using client_id. Server-side events with mismatched or missing client_id create new phantom sessions, inflating bounce rates and breaking funnel analysis.

section.title: Human-in-Loop Checkpoints

list.item: Consent banner copy and consent categories: human legal review required
PII filtering rules for server-side tagging: human privacy review required
Conversion event designation (which events count): human business review required
Production GTM container publish: human approval required

section.reference: Playwright script to verify event firing on staging via dataLayer interception before production publish
